text.title: CoT Techniques Core Rules
text.summary: Six testable rules for advanced chain-of-thought patterns.

rule.statement: Pick exactly one advanced pattern per call site: few-shot CoT OR self-consistency OR least-to-most OR tree-of-thoughts. Stacking patterns multiplies cost without proportional lift.
rule.rationale: Compounded patterns are hard to debug; pick the dominant axis (cost vs lift vs branching) and commit.

rule.statement: Zero-shot CoT baseline must be measured first. Advanced pattern is justified only if baseline accuracy < target AND failure pattern matches the candidate technique.
rule.rationale: Without baseline we cannot tell if advanced pattern is helping or just adding cost.

rule.statement: Lift gate: keep advanced pattern only if it beats baseline by ≥3 absolute pp on a ≥50-case eval. Smaller lifts are within noise floor.
rule.rationale: Lift below 3pp is statistically thin; cost is concrete (Nx); ratio is poor.

rule.statement: Cost guard: per-call cost cap configured. Self-consistency with N=5 and ToT with branch=4 depth=3 can be 4-20x baseline; cap prevents runaway.
rule.rationale: Cost guards catch a misconfigured N or branch factor before the invoice does.

rule.statement: Self-consistency requires an explicit voting rule: majority for categorical answers; median for numeric; LLM judge for free-text (judge must be calibrated per `[[judge-calibration-protocol]]`).
rule.rationale: Without a defined voting rule, ties and judge drift become silent quality bugs.

rule.statement: Tree-of-thoughts requires a branch-pruning value function (LLM scorer or programmatic). Unpruned trees explode in cost; max branches per node ≤4, max depth ≤4.
rule.rationale: Bounds prevent exponential cost blowup; pruning concentrates compute on promising paths.

rule.statement: Baseline measured + budget OK + pattern picked → wire runner and run A/B.
Zero-shot CoT already at target OR budget too tight → skip; revert to baseline.
